g.data-narration: Introduction to Programming on AWS and Azure - comprehensive overview of cloud programming concepts
Cloud programming shifts focus from managing hardware to leveraging scalable managed services for business logic
g.text: Shift from managing hardware to leveraging scalable, managed services.
Focus on business logic while cloud handles provisioning, scaling,
and maintenance. Service-oriented and event-driven models.

g.data-narration: Two main architecture models: Service-Oriented using APIs and Event-Driven where events trigger code
Shared responsibility model defines provider secures infrastructure while customer secures data and applications
IaC defines infrastructure in JSON or YAML for version control, consistency, and repeatable deployments
g.text: Define infrastructure in code (JSON/YAML) for version control,

g.data-narration: AWS core services include Lambda for serverless, S3 for storage, DynamoDB for NoSQL, and SQS for messaging
AWS Boto3 SDK for Python enables programmatic access to AWS services like listing S3 buckets
Azure SDK uses DefaultAzureCredential for authentication and BlobServiceClient for storage operations
Key service equivalents: Lambda equals Azure Functions, S3 equals Blob Storage, CloudFormation equals ARM Templates
Event-driven example: S3 upload triggers Lambda function to process image and save thumbnail back to S3
g.text: Event-Driven Example: S3 → Lambda Image Processing

g.data-narration: Best practices include designing for failure, using managed services, implementing security early, and monitoring from the start
Exam preparation tips: understand Shared Responsibility Model, know core services, and master IaC concepts
g.text: • Understand Shared Responsibility Model clearly - frequently tested
• Know core compute, storage, database services for both platforms
• IaC is key - CloudFormation and ARM Templates concepts

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: cloud programming shifts focus to business logic with managed services using event-driven and IaC approaches
g.text: Cloud programming shifts focus to business logic with managed services
AWS offers breadth; Azure excels in Microsoft ecosystem integration
